title: Modern Farmhouse Two-Tier Console Table
summary: A console with matching top and bottom shelf panels edge-glued from 1x8 boards, trimmed identically, and joined by a straight 2x4 leg frame with beveled corner braces.
description:
Both the top and bottom of this console are built the same way — edge-glue two 1x8 boards together, then trim the assembled panel on all four sides with 1x4 — so build both shelf panels identically before moving to the base. Measure the trim to fit your actual glued-up panel width rather than cutting to the plan's numbers outright, since board width varies slightly board to board.

The leg frame is four straight 2x4 legs joined by an inner support at the height of the bottom shelf, with **beveled 2x4 corner braces cut at 45° on both ends** tying each leg to the top rail — a simpler alternative to a full X-brace that still keeps the base from racking side to side. Attach the leg sets to the underside of the tabletop panel first, checking for level, then set the whole leg-and-top assembly down onto the bottom shelf panel and screw through from above.

**Finish the base by adding the remaining cross supports last**, once the top and bottom are already joined by the legs — trying to add them before the two shelves are connected makes it much harder to keep the base square.

materials.name: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

cutList.note: Cut to fit the assembled panel width.
Long point to long point, both ends cut at 45° bevel, ends not parallel.

steps.title: Cut the parts
steps.body: Cut four tabletop boards at 47-3/4" from the two 8 ft 1x8, two per board — that uses 95-5/8" of a 96" board once you allow 1/8" for the kerf, so measure your boards before cutting and start at the squarest end. From the 2x4 cut four legs at 23", two inner supports at 14-1/2" and eight cross supports at 5-1/2" long point to long point, both ends cut at 45° with the ends NOT parallel; both 8 ft 2x4 cover all fourteen pieces. The eight 1x4 trim pieces — four side trim at 14-1/2" and four front/back trim at 49-1/4" — need all three 1x4 boards, and not the way the cut list's material column suggests: the 10 ft board holds two 49-1/4" pieces plus one 14-1/2", one 8 ft board holds a 49-1/4" plus the other three 14-1/2", and the last 49-1/4" takes the second 8 ft board. Cut the trim last regardless, once the panels are glued up and measured.

steps.title: Build the top and bottom panels
steps.body: Edge-glue two 47-3/4" 1x8 boards for each panel — at 7-1/4" apiece that gives a panel 47-3/4" long and 14-1/2" wide — running glue along the mating edges and clamping or weighting them flat until dry. Build two identical panels, one for the top and one for the bottom shelf; anything different between them reads immediately on a two-tier piece where they sit one above the other. Measure each glued panel's actual width, then cut the side trim to that measurement (14-1/2" nominal) and nail it across the panel ends with 1-1/4" brads and glue. The front and back trim then runs the full length over the ends of the side trim at 49-1/4" — the 47-3/4" panel plus 3/4" of trim at each end — which caps everything and gives a finished panel 49-1/4" by 16".

steps.title: Build the leg frame
steps.body: Build two leg sets, each two 23" legs joined by a 14-1/2" inner support — the same length as the side trim, so the leg sets come out matching the panel's width. Set the support at the height you want the bottom shelf and drive two 2-1/2" screws per joint into the legs. Then add four of the eight cross supports as corner braces: each is 5-1/2" long point to long point with a 45° cut at both ends and the ends not parallel, so it spans from a leg down to the support at 45° and stops the leg set racking sideways. Fasten those with 1-1/4" brads and glue, checking each leg set sits flat and square on the floor before the glue sets. The remaining four braces stay off until the end, once the top and bottom panels are already joined.

steps.title: Attach the legs to the tabletop
steps.body: Attach the leg frame inside the top panel with screws and glue.

steps.title: Attach the bottom shelf
steps.body: Set the leg-and-top assembly on top of the bottom shelf panel. Attach with screws and glue.

steps.title: Add the remaining cross supports
steps.body: Finish the base by adding the remaining cross supports with 1-1/4" brad nails and glue.

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Sand all surfaces and apply primer and paint, or stain and poly.
materials: Primer and paint, or stain and poly
